title: test/system: quadlet - rootfs failure on kernel 7.1
body:
As reported by fedora openQA the quadlet - rootfs test case fail on the latest kernel 7.1 updates.
https://openqa.fedoraproject.org/tests/4658366/logfile?filename=podman-podman-bats.txt

```
not ok 253 |252| quadlet - rootfs

```

This seems to be due a recent change that correctly checks the backing fs selinux labels, commit `82544d36b172 selinux: fix overlayfs mmap() and mprotect() access checks`

The problem seems to be with the test, we try to access the files from another mounted container directly but they will have a different selinux level like from the quadlet container so access will be blocked.
